description: In Go, use the package-local commands (go build ./... with the module's build tags and GOOS/GOARCH) rather than a root or generic command.
body: In Go, the real contract lives in the owning package's config; a root or generic command can use the wrong the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode or skip checks. Use when building, testing, or analyzing a specific Go package, especially in a monorepo. Do not assume a top-level command covers the package, and avoid running a generic command that ignores its config. Actions: find the package's own targets, run go build ./... with the module's build tags and GOOS/GOARCH and go test ./... -race on the changed package scoped to it, and compare results against the package's configured gate. Validation signals include the package-scoped command log, its exit code, and a go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) pass using the package config. Risk boundary: escalate if no package-local command exists or if it depends on environment you cannot safely provision.
